member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator
member.summary: Provides extension methods for managing exception and exit code information within a workflow context. Enables setting, retrieving, and checking for exceptions and exit codes using well-known context property keys.
member.remarks: Use the methods in this class to record and access exception details and exit codes in a workflow's context. This facilitates consistent error handling and reporting across workflow steps. The well-known property keys defined in the nested classes ensure interoperability and standardization when storing exception-related information.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator.WellKnown
member.summary: Provides well-known constants for use in application context and metadata keys.
member.remarks: The constants defined in this class are intended to standardize context keys across the application, reducing the risk of errors from hard-coded strings. Use these values when storing or retrieving context information such as exception details or process exit codes.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator.FailureMessage(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Retrieves the failure message from the context, providing details about the most deeply nested exception if present.
member.remarks: Use this method to obtain a user-friendly error message when handling failures in operations that utilize the context. If multiple nested exceptions are present, only the innermost exception's message is returned.
member.param: The context from which to extract the failure message. Must not be null and should provide exception information via HasException and GetException methods.
member.returns: A string containing the message of the innermost exception if an exception exists in the context; otherwise, "Unknown failure.".

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator.HasException(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Determines whether the context contains an associated exception.
member.remarks: Use this method to verify if an exception has been recorded in the context before attempting to retrieve or handle it.
member.param: The context to check for an exception. Cannot be null.
member.returns: true if the context contains an exception; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator.HasExitCode(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Determines whether the context contains an exit code property.
member.param: The context to check for the presence of an exit code property. Cannot be null.
member.returns: true if the context contains an exit code property; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator.GetException(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Retrieves the exception associated with the specified context.
member.param: The context from which to obtain the exception. Must not be null and must contain an exception.
member.returns: The exception stored in the context.
member.exception: Thrown if the context does not contain an exception.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator.GetExitCode(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Retrieves the exit code from the specified context.
member.param: The context from which to obtain the exit code. Must support exit code retrieval.
member.returns: The exit code value associated with the context.
member.exception: Thrown if the context does not contain an exit code.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator.SetException(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext,System.Exception)
member.summary: Associates the specified exception with the given context instance.
member.remarks: Use this method to record an exception in the context for later retrieval or handling. This is useful for propagating error information through context-aware operations.
member.param: The context in which to store the exception. Cannot be null.
The exception to associate with the context. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator.SetException(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext,System.Int32,System.Exception)
member.summary: Sets the exception and exit code properties in the specified context to indicate an error state.
member.remarks: This method updates the context's properties to reflect an error, allowing downstream consumers to access the exception and exit code. Use this method to signal failure or error conditions within the context.
member.param: The context in which to set the exception and exit code properties. Cannot be null.
The exit code representing the error condition to associate with the context.
The exception to associate with the context. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator.SetException(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext,System.String)
member.summary: Sets an exception on the specified context using the provided error message.
member.param: The context in which to set the exception. Cannot be null.
The error message to associate with the exception. Cannot be null or empty.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator.SetException(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Sets an exception and exit code in the specified context, indicating an error condition with a custom message.
member.remarks: This method updates the context to reflect an error state, allowing downstream consumers to detect and handle failures based on the exception and exit code.
member.param: The context in which to record the exception and exit code. Cannot be null.
The exit code to associate with the error. Typically used to indicate the reason for failure.
The error message to include in the exception. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator.SetException(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext,System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Sets an exception on the specified context using a formatted message.
member.remarks: The exception message is created using the specified format string and arguments. This method is useful for providing detailed error information in the context.
member.param: The context on which to set the exception. Cannot be null.
A composite format string that specifies the exception message.
An array of objects to format into the exception message.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ExceptionContextDecorator.SetException(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext,System.Int32,System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Sets an exception and exit code on the specified context using a formatted error message.
member.remarks: This method creates an exception with a formatted message and sets it on the context, along with the provided exit code. Use this to signal errors and communicate exit codes in a standardized way.
member.param: The context in which to set the exception and exit code. Cannot be null.
The exit code to associate with the exception. Typically used to indicate the error type or severity.
A composite format string that defines the error message.
An array of objects to format into the error message string.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ServiceCollectionDecorator
member.summary: Provides extension methods for configuring workflow-related services in an IServiceCollection.
member.remarks: This class enables registration of workflow factories and managers for dependency injection. Use its methods to add workflow functionality to your application's service container.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.ServiceCollectionDecorator.UseWorkflowFactory(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Registers workflow-related factories with the dependency injection container.
member.remarks: This method adds transient implementations for workflow manager and workflow item factories. Use this extension when configuring services to enable workflow functionality in your application.
member.param: The service collection to which workflow factory services will be added. Cannot be null.
member.returns: The updated service collection with workflow factory services registered.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.MissingStepsException
member.summary: Represents an exception that is thrown when no workflow steps are found for the specified group.
member.remarks: This exception is typically used to indicate a configuration or runtime error in workflow processing when a required group of steps is missing. It derives from WorkflowException and provides a message identifying the missing group.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.MissingStepsException.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the MissingStepsException class with a specified workflow group name.
member.remarks: This exception is typically thrown when a workflow operation expects steps to be defined for a given group, but none are present. Use this exception to signal missing configuration or incomplete workflow definitions.
member.param: The name of the workflow group for which no steps were found. Cannot be null or empty.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.NoExceptionException
member.summary: Represents an error that occurs when an expected exception object is not found in the context properties.
member.remarks: This exception is typically thrown when an operation requires an exception object to be present in the context, but none is available. Use this exception to signal missing exception information in custom error handling scenarios.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.NoExceptionException.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the NoExceptionException class, indicating that an exception object was not found in the context properties.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.NoExitCodeException
member.summary: Represents an error that occurs when an expected exit code is missing from the context properties.
member.remarks: This exception is typically thrown when an operation requires an exit code to be present in the context, but none is found. Use this exception to signal that the exit code retrieval failed due to its absence.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.NoExitCodeException.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the NoExitCodeException class, indicating that an exit code was not found in the context properties.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.UnknownFailureException
member.summary: Represents an exception that is thrown when a workflow step fails for an unknown reason.
member.remarks: This exception is typically used to indicate that a workflow step encountered an error that could not be classified or handled by more specific exception types. It provides information about the step name and step number to aid in troubleshooting.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.UnknownFailureException.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the UnknownFailureException class for a specific step that failed due to an unknown reason.
member.param: The name of the step that encountered the failure.
The sequence number of the step that failed.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowException
member.summary: Represents errors that occur during workflow execution.
member.remarks: This is the base class for exceptions thrown by workflow-related operations. Use this type to catch and handle workflow-specific errors separately from other exception types. Derived classes provide more specific error information for various workflow failure scenarios.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowException.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowException class.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowException.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowException class with a specified error message.
member.param: The message that describes the error. This value is passed to the base Exception class and can be accessed via the Message property.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowException.#ctor(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowException class with a specified error message and a reference to the inner exception that is the cause of this exception.
member.param: The error message that explains the reason for the exception.
The exception that is the cause of the current exception, or null if no inner exception is specified.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowFailureException
member.summary: Represents an exception that is thrown when a workflow fails at a specific step.
member.remarks: This exception provides information about the workflow step where the failure occurred, including the step name and number. The underlying cause of the failure is available in the inner exception.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowFailureException.#ctor(System.Exception,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowFailureException class with a specified inner exception, step name, and step number indicating where the workflow failed.
member.remarks: Use this constructor to provide detailed context about workflow failures, including the specific step and underlying exception. The step information can help diagnose and resolve issues in complex workflows.
member.param: The exception that caused the workflow to fail. Cannot be null.
The name of the workflow step where the failure occurred.
The zero-based index of the workflow step where the failure occurred.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowManagerNotFoundException
member.summary: Represents an exception that is thrown when a workflow manager for a specified group name has not been configured.
member.remarks: This exception is typically thrown when attempting to access a workflow manager instance that does not exist for the given group name. Use this exception to identify configuration issues related to workflow manager registration.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowManagerNotFoundException.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowManagerNotFoundException class with the specified workflow manager group name.
member.param: The name of the workflow manager group that was not found. Cannot be null or empty.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemFactory
member.summary: Provides functionality to retrieve and manage workflow items using dependency injection and logging services.
member.remarks: The factory uses the provided service provider to resolve workflow item collections based on a service key. Logging is performed for workflow item discovery and processing. This class is intended to be used as a base for custom workflow item factories and supports extensibility through virtual members.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemFactory.#ctor(System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowItemFactory class using the specified service provider.
member.param: The service provider used to resolve dependencies required by the factory. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemFactory.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemFactory},System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowItemFactory class using the specified service provider and logger.
member.param: The service provider used to resolve dependencies required by the factory.
The logger used to record diagnostic and operational information for the factory.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemFactory.ServiceProvider
member.summary: Gets or sets the service provider used to resolve service dependencies within the class.
member.remarks: Assigning a custom service provider allows for advanced dependency injection scenarios or integration with external service containers.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemFactory.Logger
member.summary: Gets or sets the logger instance used for logging workflow item factory operations.
member.remarks: Assign a custom logger to capture log messages generated by the workflow item factory. By default, logging is disabled unless a logger is provided.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemFactory.GetItemsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Asynchronously retrieves workflow items associated with the specified service key.
member.remarks: Only items that implement the IWorkflowItem interface and match the expected type are included in the result. Items that do not match are disposed if possible.
member.param: The unique key identifying the service for which workflow items are requested. Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result contains a collection of workflow items matching the specified service key.
member.exception: Thrown if no workflow items have been configured for the specified service key.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowManagerFactory
member.summary: This is a generic repository factory that can return a repository for any given entity interface.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowManagerFactory.#ctor(System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowManagerFactory class using the specified service provider.
member.param: The service provider used to resolve dependencies required by the factory. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowManagerFactory.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowManagerFactory},System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowManagerFactory class with the specified service provider and logger.
member.param: The service provider used to resolve dependencies required by the factory.
The logger used to record diagnostic and operational information for the factory.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowManagerFactory.ServiceProvider
member.summary: Gets or sets the service provider used to resolve service dependencies within the class.
member.remarks: Assigning a custom service provider allows for advanced dependency injection scenarios, such as substituting services during testing or extending functionality. The property is typically used by derived classes to access or override service resolution behavior.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowManagerFactory.Logger
member.summary: Gets or sets the logger used for logging workflow manager factory operations.
member.remarks: Assign a custom logger to capture diagnostic information or errors during workflow manager factory execution. By default, logging is disabled unless a logger is provided.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowManagerFactory.GetAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Asynchronously retrieves the workflow manager associated with the specified service key.
member.remarks: This method searches for a workflow manager registered with the given service key. If no matching manager is found, an exception is thrown. The operation completes synchronously but returns a task for compatibility with asynchronous patterns.
member.param: The unique key identifying the workflow manager to retrieve. Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result contains the workflow manager instance associated with the specified service key.
member.exception: Thrown if no workflow manager is found for the specified service key.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearCompleteWorkflowManager
member.summary: This workflow manager executes ALL steps in a work flow. If the current step indicates it should not be executed it is skipped and the work flow moves on to the next step.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearCompleteWorkflowManager.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearCompleteWorkflowManager},Diamond.Core.Workflow.IWorkflowItemFactory)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the LinearCompleteWorkflowManager class with the specified logger and workflow item factory.
member.param: The logger used to record diagnostic and operational information for the workflow manager.
The factory used to create workflow items managed by this instance.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearCompleteWorkflowManager.#ctor(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IWorkflowItemFactory)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the LinearCompleteWorkflowManager class using the specified workflow item factory.
member.param: The factory used to create workflow items for the manager. Cannot be null.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearCompleteWorkflowManager.WorkflowItemFactory
member.summary: Gets or sets the factory used to create workflow items.
member.remarks: Assigning a custom factory allows for customization of workflow item creation. This property is typically used to inject specialized behavior or dependencies into workflow items.

member.name: F:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearCompleteWorkflowManager._steps
member.summary: Backing field for the property. This field holds the collection of workflow steps for the current instance.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearCompleteWorkflowManager.Steps
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of workflow steps for the current instance.
member.remarks: The steps must be numbered contiguously, starting with 1. Setting this property with steps that do not meet these requirements will result in an exception. The steps are stored in order based on their ordinal values.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearCompleteWorkflowManager.Logger
member.summary: Gets or sets the logger used to record diagnostic and operational information for the workflow manager.
member.remarks: Assigning a custom logger allows integration with different logging frameworks or configuration of log output. By default, logging is disabled unless a logger is explicitly provided.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearCompleteWorkflowManager.ServiceKey
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique key used to identify the service instance.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearCompleteWorkflowManager.ExecuteWorkflowAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Executes the workflow asynchronously, processing each step in sequence and updating the workflow state in the provided context.
member.remarks: The method updates context properties to reflect the outcome of each step and the overall workflow. Logging is performed for progress and step results. The workflow is executed step-by-step, and failure in any step will mark the workflow as failed.
member.param: The context object used to track workflow state and properties throughout execution. Cannot be null.
member.returns: A value indicating whether the workflow completed successfully. Returns if all steps succeeded; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearCompleteWorkflowManager.LoadAsync
member.summary: Asynchronously loads workflow steps if they have not already been loaded.
member.remarks: This method should be called before accessing workflow steps to ensure they are loaded. If steps are already loaded, the method completes without reloading.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous load operation.
member.exception: Thrown if no steps are found for the workflow associated with the current service key.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearCompleteWorkflowManager.ExecuteStepAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IWorkflowItem,Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Executes the specified workflow step asynchronously within the given context.
member.remarks: If the step fails, the method sets an appropriate exception in the context to indicate the failure reason. Logging is performed for both successful and failed executions.
member.param: The workflow item to execute. Represents a single step in the workflow.
The execution context for the workflow step. Provides state and exception handling for the step.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is if the step completed successfully; otherwise, .

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager
member.summary: Provides workflow management for executing a sequence of workflow steps in a linear fashion, halting execution upon the first failure. Supports customization of workflow item creation and logging.
member.remarks: Use this class to manage workflows where steps must be executed in order and the workflow should stop if any step fails. The manager ensures steps are numbered contiguously and allows for integration with custom logging and workflow item factories. The final step can be configured to always execute, regardless of previous failures. Thread safety is not guaranteed; external synchronization may be required for concurrent access.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager.#ctor(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IWorkflowItemFactory,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the LinearHaltWorkflowManager class with the specified workflow item factory and logger.
member.param: The factory used to create workflow items for the manager. Cannot be null.
The logger used to record diagnostic and operational information for the manager. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager.#ctor(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IWorkflowItemFactory)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the LinearHaltWorkflowManager class using the specified workflow item factory.
member.param: The factory used to create workflow items for the manager. Cannot be null.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager.WorkflowItemFactory
member.summary: Gets or sets the factory used to create workflow items.
member.remarks: Assigning a custom factory allows for customization of workflow item creation. This property is typically used to inject specialized behavior or dependencies into workflow items.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager.Logger
member.summary: Gets or sets the logger used to record diagnostic and operational information for the workflow manager.
member.remarks: Assigning a custom logger allows integration with different logging frameworks or configuration of log output. By default, logging is disabled unless a logger is explicitly provided.

member.name: F:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager._steps
member.summary: Backing field for the property. This field holds the collection of workflow steps for the current instance.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager.Steps
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of workflow steps for the current instance.
member.remarks: The steps must be numbered contiguously, starting with 1. Setting this property with steps that do not meet these requirements will result in an exception. The steps are stored in order based on their ordinal values.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager.ServiceKey
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique key used to identify the service instance.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager.ExecuteWorkflowAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Executes the workflow asynchronously, processing each step in sequence and updating the workflow state based on step outcomes.
member.remarks: If a step fails, subsequent steps are not executed except for any step marked to always execute as the final step. The workflow context properties are updated to reflect the outcome of the execution.
member.param: The workflow execution context containing properties and state information used during workflow processing. Cannot be null.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is if all workflow steps completed successfully; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager.ExecuteStepAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IWorkflowItem,Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Executes the specified workflow step asynchronously within the given context and determines whether the step completed successfully.
member.remarks: If the step fails or an exception occurs during execution, the context is updated with the relevant exception information. Override this method to customize step execution behavior in derived classes.
member.param: The workflow item to execute. Represents a single step in the workflow process.
The execution context for the workflow step. Provides state and exception handling for the step execution.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is if the step executed successfully; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager.LoadAsync
member.summary: Asynchronously loads workflow steps for the current group if they have not already been loaded.
member.remarks: Override this method to customize how workflow steps are loaded for a group. Steps are only loaded if they have not been previously set.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous load operation.
member.exception: Thrown if no steps are found for the specified group.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager.AlwaysExecuteStepIndex
member.summary: Gets the index of the step that is always executed, if present, in the sequence of steps.
member.remarks: Returns the index of the last step if it is marked to always execute; otherwise, returns -1. This property is useful for identifying special steps that must run regardless of other conditions.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager.HasAlwaysExecuteStep
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the last step in the sequence is configured to always execute.
member.remarks: Use this property to determine if the final step will run regardless of previous outcomes. This can be useful for ensuring cleanup or finalization actions are performed.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.LinearHaltWorkflowManager.FinalStepOfWorkflow
member.summary: Gets the index of the final step in the workflow sequence.
member.remarks: If the workflow contains an always-execute step marked as final, the index returned corresponds to the step immediately preceding it. This property is intended for scenarios where the workflow's completion logic depends on identifying the last actionable step.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowContext
member.summary: Defines a generic context that can be used for a workflow. The context allows data to be shared between the multiple steps of a workflow during execution.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowContext.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name associated with this instance. The name is used for logging.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowContext.Arguments
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of command-line arguments associated with the current operation.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowContext.Properties
member.summary: Gets the collection of state properties associated with the current context.
member.remarks: Use this property to store and retrieve custom data relevant to the context. The collection persists for the lifetime of the context and can be used to share information between components.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowContext.ResetAsync
member.summary: Resets the current instance asynchronously to its initial state.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous reset operation.
member.exception: Thrown if the method is not implemented in a derived class.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate
member.summary: Represents an abstract template for a workflow item, providing base functionality for execution, logging, grouping, and ordering within a workflow. Derived classes implement specific step behavior by overriding execution methods.
member.remarks: This class defines the structure and lifecycle for workflow steps, including preparation, conditional execution, post-execution actions, and error handling. It is intended to be inherited by concrete workflow item implementations. The workflow manager typically orchestrates the execution of these items. Thread safety depends on the implementation of derived classes and the logger provided.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowItemTemplate class.
member.remarks: The Name property is set to the class name with the 'Step' suffix removed during initialization. This can be useful for identifying workflow items by their template type.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowItemTemplate class using the specified logger for diagnostic output.
member.param: The logger instance used to record diagnostic information and operational events for the WorkflowItemTemplate.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate},System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowItemTemplate class with the specified name, group, and ordinal value.
member.param: The logger used for logging diagnostic and operational information within the WorkflowItemTemplate instance. Cannot be null.
The name assigned to the workflow item template. Cannot be null or empty.
The ordinal value that determines the order of the workflow item template within its group. Must be a non-negative integer.

members.member:
Creates an instance of with the given logger, name, group ordinal and value for the property.
An instance of the logger used by this step to create log entries during execution. The name of the step. The order in the group this step executes. Sets the property.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.Name
member.summary: Gets/sets the name of this workflow item for logging purposes.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.Ordinal
member.summary: The order this item appears in the execution steps.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.AlwaysExecute
member.summary: Gets/sets value to indicate that this step should always execute. This does not override and is used by some workflow managers but not all.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.Weight
member.summary: Gets/sets a weigh used for determining priority of this step. This value is used by some workflow managers but not all.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.Logger
member.summary: Gets/sets the instance of the logger used by this step to create log entries during execution.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.ExecuteStepAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Performs all of the steps of this step's execution including calls to , and . This is usually called by the workflow manager.
member.param: The current workflow context.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.OnPrepareForExecutionAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Called prior to and to prepare the step for execution.
member.param: The current workflow context.
member.returns: Returns true if preparation for the step execution was successful; false otherwise.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.OnShouldExecuteAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Allows the step to dynamically defer execution based on the context.
member.param: The current workflow context.
member.returns: Returns true if the step should execute, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.OnPostExecutionAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Called after a step has executed to perform any necessary cleanup.
member.param: The current workflow context.
member.returns: Returns true is the post execution actions were successful; false otherwise.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.OnExecuteStepAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Performs the actual work of the step execution. This should be overwritten in the concrete class to perform the necessary actions.
member.param: The current workflow context.
member.returns: Returns true if the step executed successfully; false otherwise.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.StepFailedAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext,System.String)
member.summary: Marks a step as failed and provides an error message.
member.param: The current workflow context.
The message that will be logged to the logging system and the workflow context.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate.ToString
member.summary: Returns a string representation of this step.
member.returns: Returns a string representation of this step.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.CreateTemporaryFolderStep
member.summary: Represents a workflow step that creates a temporary folder and stores it in the workflow context.
member.remarks: This step is typically used in automated workflows that require a dedicated temporary storage location. The created folder is accessible to subsequent workflow steps via the context properties. Thread safety depends on the implementation of the underlying temporary folder factory.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.CreateTemporaryFolderStep.#ctor(Diamond.Core.System.TemporaryFolder.ITemporaryFolderFactory)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the CreateTemporaryFolderStep class using the specified temporary folder factory.
member.param: The factory used to create temporary folders for this step. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.CreateTemporaryFolderStep.#ctor(Diamond.Core.System.TemporaryFolder.ITemporaryFolderFactory,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the CreateTemporaryFolderStep class with the specified temporary folder factory and logger.
member.param: The factory used to create temporary folders for workflow operations. Cannot be null.
The logger used to record diagnostic information and workflow events. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.CreateTemporaryFolderStep.#ctor(Diamond.Core.System.TemporaryFolder.ITemporaryFolderFactory,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate},System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the CreateTemporaryFolderStep class with the specified folder factory, logger, name, group, and ordinal.
member.param: The factory used to create temporary folders for this workflow step. Cannot be null.
The logger instance used for logging workflow item template operations. Cannot be null.
The name assigned to this workflow step. Cannot be null or empty.
The ordinal position of this step within the workflow. Must be a non-negative integer.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.CreateTemporaryFolderStep.#ctor(Diamond.Core.System.TemporaryFolder.ITemporaryFolderFactory,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate},System.String,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the CreateTemporaryFolderStep class with the specified configuration and execution behavior.
member.param: The factory used to create temporary folders for the workflow step. Cannot be null.
The logger instance used for logging workflow item template events. Cannot be null.
The name assigned to the workflow step. Used for identification within the workflow.
The ordinal position of the step within the workflow sequence. Must be a non-negative integer.
A value indicating whether the step should always execute, regardless of workflow conditions. Set to to force execution.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.CreateTemporaryFolderStep.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the display name for the operation.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.CreateTemporaryFolderStep.TemporaryFolderFactory
member.summary: Gets or sets the factory used to create temporary folders for file operations.
member.remarks: Override this property to customize how temporary folders are created or managed. The factory should implement strategies appropriate for the application's environment and requirements.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.CreateTemporaryFolderStep.OnExecuteStepAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Executes the step by creating a temporary folder and updating the context with its reference.
member.remarks: If the temporary folder cannot be created, the step is marked as failed and the context is not updated. The method logs the creation of the folder for diagnostic purposes.
member.param: The workflow context used to store properties and track execution state.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is if the temporary folder was successfully created and set in the context; otherwise, .

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DeleteTemporaryFolderStep
member.summary: Represents a workflow step that deletes a temporary folder created during the workflow execution.
member.remarks: This step is typically used to clean up temporary resources at the end of a workflow. It relies on an implementation of ITemporaryFolderFactory to manage folder creation and deletion. The step logs the deletion process and ensures the temporary folder is removed from the workflow context. If the folder cannot be deleted, a warning is logged. This class can be customized with additional parameters such as name, group, ordinal, and alwaysExecute to fit specific workflow requirements.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DeleteTemporaryFolderStep.#ctor(Diamond.Core.System.TemporaryFolder.ITemporaryFolderFactory)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the DeleteTemporaryFolderStep class using the specified temporary folder factory.
member.param: The factory used to create and manage temporary folders for this step. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DeleteTemporaryFolderStep.#ctor(Diamond.Core.System.TemporaryFolder.ITemporaryFolderFactory,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the DeleteTemporaryFolderStep class with the specified temporary folder factory and logger.
member.param: The factory used to create and manage temporary folders for workflow operations. Cannot be null.
The logger used to record diagnostic information and workflow events. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DeleteTemporaryFolderStep.#ctor(Diamond.Core.System.TemporaryFolder.ITemporaryFolderFactory,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate},System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the DeleteTemporaryFolderStep class with the specified folder factory, logger, name, group, and ordinal position.
member.param: The factory used to create and manage temporary folders for the workflow step.
The logger instance used to record diagnostic and operational information for the workflow item template.
The name assigned to this workflow step. Cannot be null or empty.
The ordinal position of this step within the workflow sequence. Must be a non-negative integer.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DeleteTemporaryFolderStep.#ctor(Diamond.Core.System.TemporaryFolder.ITemporaryFolderFactory,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Diamond.Core.Workflow.WorkflowItemTemplate},System.String,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the DeleteTemporaryFolderStep class with the specified configuration and execution behavior.
member.param: The factory used to create and manage temporary folders for workflow operations.
The logger instance used to record diagnostic and workflow-related messages.
The name assigned to this workflow step. Used for identification within the workflow.
The ordinal position of this step in the workflow sequence. Determines execution order.
A value indicating whether this step should execute regardless of workflow conditions. Set to to force execution.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DeleteTemporaryFolderStep.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the display name for the operation.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DeleteTemporaryFolderStep.TemporaryFolderFactory
member.summary: Gets or sets the factory used to create temporary folders for file operations.
member.remarks: Override this property to customize how temporary folders are created or managed. The factory should implement strategies for creating, cleaning up, and managing temporary storage as needed by the application.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DeleteTemporaryFolderStep.OnExecuteStepAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Executes the step to delete the temporary folder associated with the current workflow context.
member.remarks: If a temporary folder is present in the context, it is disposed and removed. Logging is performed to indicate whether the folder was successfully deleted. The method always returns .
member.param: The workflow context containing properties and state for the current execution step. Must not be null.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is when the step completes.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.IsErrorStep
member.summary: Represents a workflow step that checks whether the workflow is in an error state.
member.remarks: This class is used within a workflow to determine if an error has occurred and to handle error conditions appropriately. If an error is detected, the workflow step will fail and provide an error message if available. Otherwise, the workflow continues as normal.

member.name: P:Diamond.Core.Workflow.IsErrorStep.Name
member.summary: Gets the display name for the error state of the workflow.

member.name: M:Diamond.Core.Workflow.IsErrorStep.OnExecuteStepAsync(Diamond.Core.Workflow.IContext)
member.summary: Executes the workflow step asynchronously and determines whether the workflow should continue based on the error state in the context.
member.remarks: If the context contains a workflow error, the step is marked as failed and the workflow will not continue. If no error is present, the workflow proceeds to the next step.
member.param: The workflow execution context containing properties that influence step execution, including error information.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is if the workflow should continue; otherwise, .

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DiamondWorkflow
member.summary: Provides well-known constants and workflow context keys used throughout the Diamond workflow system.
member.remarks: This class contains nested static classes and string constants that represent standard context keys and identifiers for workflow operations. Use these constants to ensure consistency when accessing or setting workflow-related context values.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DiamondWorkflow.WellKnown
member.summary: Provides well-known constants for use in workflow context and state management scenarios.
member.remarks: The constants defined in this class represent standard keys commonly used to store and retrieve workflow-related information, such as error states, success indicators, and temporary data. These values help ensure consistency when interacting with workflow context dictionaries or state objects across different components.

member.name: T:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DiamondWorkflow.WellKnown.Context
member.summary: Provides well-known keys for storing and retrieving workflow-related context information in a state dictionary or similar data structure.
member.remarks: Use these constants to ensure consistent access to workflow state, error details, and temporary resources across workflow steps. The keys are intended for use in scenarios where workflow execution state, error handling, and temporary data management are required.

member.name: F:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DiamondWorkflow.WellKnown.Context.LastStepSuccess
member.summary: Represents the key used to indicate whether the last step was successful.

member.name: F:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DiamondWorkflow.WellKnown.Context.WorkflowError
member.summary: Represents the event name used to indicate a workflow error.

member.name: F:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DiamondWorkflow.WellKnown.Context.Exception
member.summary: Represents the string value used to identify exception-related log entries or categories.

member.name: F:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DiamondWorkflow.WellKnown.Context.WorkflowErrorMessage
member.summary: Represents the key used to identify workflow error messages in configuration or data stores.

member.name: F:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DiamondWorkflow.WellKnown.Context.WorkflowFailed
member.summary: Represents the event name used to indicate that a workflow has failed.

member.name: F:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DiamondWorkflow.WellKnown.Context.TemporaryFolder
member.summary: Represents the name of the temporary folder used for storing transient files or data.

member.name: F:Diamond.Core.Workflow.DiamondWorkflow.WellKnown.Context.IStateDictionaryArray
member.summary: Represents the name of the array type for state dictionaries used in serialization or type identification scenarios.
